governing_requirement: Mode A must read an immutable adopted package through exact source/package/mask/release/instance/owner/ontology/transform/revocation validation, cap raw manifest/status/reference evidence at noncertified, require a separate current exact-output operational wrapper for production use, and expose no package mutation path.
prerequisites: MF-P6-09.05 has no accepted independent use-eligibility decision.
MF-P6-11.01 has no accepted external adapter/conformance boundary.
MF-P6-08.05 has no accepted production operational-certificate issuance policy implementation.
verified_foundations: Package resolution constrains image IDs/person indices, selects explicit pN instance directories, requires a manifest, and rejects manifest-declared mask paths that are absolute or escape the package root.
Atomic-mask reads enforce source/mask dimension equality and strict binary values; newer unsupported package majors fail.
The node module contains no package write calls and deliberate workflow outputs inside the package truth root are rejected.
The browser can filter person-instance pairs by declared status and ontology version, and multi-person fixture reads remain distinct.
Frozen operational-certificate and bridge schemas provide a future exact-output authority vocabulary, but they are not connected to this read path.

commands: in-memory rejected-status direct-load and changed-mask-byte probes

direct_probes.mutation: Change p0 left_forearm status from human_approved_gold to rejected_needs_fix, then call MFLoadGoldMask directly by image_id/person_index/label.
direct_probes.gap: Browser filtering is not an authority gate; direct loading ignores part status and has no independent intended-use decision or wrapper check.

direct_probes.mutation: Replace left_forearm.png with a same-dimension strict-binary all-foreground mask while leaving the manifest unchanged.
direct_probes.gap: Actual source and mask bytes are not bound to a package/release/receipt/certificate identity before use.

confirmed_gaps: The loader trusts a mutable configured packages_root rather than an adopted immutable release/package catalog.
Direct load bypasses browser status filtering; raw human_approved_gold is also presented as a selectable status even though raw status must remain noncertified.
No source encoded/decoded pixel hash, mask hash, package hash, release/capability/adoption identity, or actual-byte catalog is verified.
No declared character/prop/environment owner roster, canonical scene instance, assignment evidence, character revision, or person ownership is validated beyond directory pN.
No full-frame/crop/resize/pad/flip transform chain, coordinate spaces, dimensions, inverse, or round-trip evidence is verified.
No current revocation journal/checkpoint/head is read and no stale, superseded, invalidated, or withdrawn scope is rejected.
No separate exact operational wrapper is resolved, signature/trust/validity/scope/revocation checked, or independently matched to original package artifacts and intended use.
Derived union/projected/inpaint artifacts can be loaded without proving their lineage or enforcing the parent authority ceiling.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement an additive producer Mode A read decision over adopted immutable package bytes and a separately validated exact operational wrapper, returning typed noncertified/eligible/reject evidence without changing frozen contracts or package bytes.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. Candidate paths are read-only. The scope is disjoint from preserved MF-P6-08.05 dirty paths, frozen v1 contracts, and prepared predecessor units.
acceptance_criteria: Resolve only a clean adopted release/package catalog and recompute every source encoded/decoded, mask, manifest, release, capability, ontology, policy, provider, and revocation hash from contained regular bytes.
Validate exact image/media/frame, scene/shot/take, character revision, canonical instance, declared owner roster, person-index assignment, label/intent, and protected-region scope.
Execute and verify the exact typed transform chain, dimensions, orientation, side swaps, bounds, inverse, and round-trip tolerance before returning pixels.
Treat all raw manifests, filenames, review statuses, certificate references, and missing wrappers as noncertified; no caller flag or status string may upgrade authority.

Production eligibility requires a separate trusted, active, exact-output operational wrapper whose source/mask/package/owner/transform/ontology/policy/use scope and revocation state match current observed bytes.
Derived/refined/projected/inpaint descendants remain at or below parent authority and require their own exact wrapper when production authority is requested.
Return a strict self-hashed read decision with actual-byte observations, wrapper validation, authority ceiling, eligibility, reasons, and immutable artifact handles; expose no write method.

Negative tests cover rejected/raw status direct access, path escape/link tricks, byte drift, stale/out-of-scope/revoked wrapper, wrong owner/person/revision, transform drift, derived escalation, mutation attempts, and concurrent invalidation.
Frozen v1 contracts, packages, queued/held records, and preserved dirty paths remain byte-identical.

claim_boundary: This audit credits read-only node, safe manifest-path, shape, binary, and version checks only. It does not authorize any package for production use, validate an operational wrapper, prove immutable bytes/revocation/ownership/transforms, or complete MF-P6-11.02.
